title: CurveEvaluator2D Object
description: 2D curve evaluator that is obtained from a transient curve and allows you to perform various evaluations on the curve.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: getCurvature
methods.description: Get the curvature value at a parameter position on the curve.

methods.name: getCurvatures
methods.description: Get the curvature values at a number of parameter positions on the curve.

methods.name: getEndPoints
methods.description: Get the end points of the curve.

methods.name: getFirstDerivative
methods.description: Get the first derivative of the curve at the specified parameter position.

methods.name: getFirstDerivatives
methods.description: Get the first derivatives of the curve at the specified parameter positions.

methods.name: getLengthAtParameter
methods.description: Get the length of the curve between two parameter positions on the curve.

methods.name: getParameterAtLength
methods.description: Get the parameter position on the curve that is the specified curve length from the specified starting parameter position.

methods.name: getParameterAtPoint
methods.description: Get the parameter position that correspond to a point on the curve. For reliable results, the point should lie on the curve within model tolerance. If the point does not lie on the curve, the parameter of the nearest point on the curve will generally be returned.

methods.name: getParameterExtents
methods.description: Get the parametric range of the curve.

methods.name: getParametersAtPoints
methods.description: Get the parameter positions that correspond to a set of points on the curve. For reliable results, the points should lie on the curve within model tolerance. If the points do not lie on the curve, the parameter of the nearest point on the curve will generally be returned.

methods.name: getPointAtParameter
methods.description: Get the point on the curve that corresponds to evaluating a parameter position on the curve.

methods.name: getPointsAtParameters
methods.description: Get the points on the curve that correspond to evaluating a set of parameter positions on the curve.

methods.name: getSecondDerivative
methods.description: Get the second derivative of the curve at the specified parameter position.

methods.name: getSecondDerivatives
methods.description: Get the second derivatives of the curve at the specified parameter positions.

methods.name: getStrokes
methods.description: Get a sequence of points between two curve parameter positions. The points will be a linear interpolation along the curve between these two parameter positions where the maximum deviation between the curve and each line segment will not exceed the specified tolerance value.

methods.name: getTangent
methods.description: Get the tangent to the curve at a parameter position on the curve.

methods.name: getTangents
methods.description: Get the tangent to the curve at a number of parameter positions on the curve.

methods.name: getThirdDerivative
methods.description: Get the third derivative of the curve at the specified parameter position.

methods.name: getThirdDerivatives
methods.description: Get the third derivatives of the curve at the specified parameter positions.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

classType.title: CurveEvaluator2D.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

getCurvature.title: CurveEvaluator2D.getCurvature Method
getCurvature.description: Get the curvature value at a parameter position on the curve.
getCurvature.return_description: Returns true if the curvature was successfully returned.

parameters.name: parameter
parameters.description: The parameter position to return the curvature information at. This value must be within the range of the parameter extents as provided by getParameterExtents.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: The output direction of the curvature at the position on the curve.

parameters.name: curvature
parameters.description: The output magnitude of the curvature at the position on the curve.

getCurvatures.title: CurveEvaluator2D.getCurvatures Method
getCurvatures.description: Get the curvature values at a number of parameter positions on the curve.
getCurvatures.return_description: Returns true if the curvatures were successfully returned.

parameters.name: parameters
parameters.description: The array of parameter positions to return curvature information at. Each parameter value must be within the range of the parameter extents as provided by getParameterExtents.

parameters.name: directions
parameters.description: The output array of the direction of the curvature at each position on the curve. The length of this array will be the same as the length of the parameters array provided.

parameters.name: curvatures
parameters.description: The output array of the magnitude of the curvature at the position on the curve. The length of this array will be the same as the length of the parameters array provided.

getEndPoints.title: CurveEvaluator2D.getEndPoints Method
getEndPoints.description: Get the end points of the curve.
getEndPoints.return_description: Returns true if the end points were successfully returned.

parameters.name: startPoint
parameters.description: The output start point of the curve. If the curve is unbounded at the start, this value will be null.

parameters.name: endPoint
parameters.description: The output end point of the curve. If the curve is unbounded at the end, this value will be null.

getFirstDerivative.title: CurveEvaluator2D.getFirstDerivative Method
getFirstDerivative.description: Get the first derivative of the curve at the specified parameter position.
getFirstDerivative.return_description: Returns true if the first derivative was successfully returned.

parameters.name: parameter
parameters.description: The parameter position to get the curve first derivative at. The parameter value must be within the range of the parameter extents as provided by getParameterExtents.

parameters.name: firstDerivative
parameters.description: The output first derivative vector at the parameter position specified.

getFirstDerivatives.title: CurveEvaluator2D.getFirstDerivatives Method
getFirstDerivatives.description: Get the first derivatives of the curve at the specified parameter positions.
getFirstDerivatives.return_description: Returns true if the first derivatives were successfully returned.

parameters.name: parameters
parameters.description: The array of parameter positions to get the curve first derivative at. Each parameter value must be within the range of the parameter extents as provided by getParameterExtents.

parameters.name: firstDerivatives
parameters.description: The output array of first derivative vectors at each parameter position specified. The length of this array is equal to the length of the parameters array specified.

getLengthAtParameter.title: CurveEvaluator2D.getLengthAtParameter Method
getLengthAtParameter.description: Get the length of the curve between two parameter positions on the curve.
getLengthAtParameter.return_description: Returns true if the length was successfully returned.

parameters.name: fromParameter
parameters.description: The parameter position to measure the curve length from. This value must be within the range of the parameter extents as provided by getParameterExtents.

parameters.name: toParameter
parameters.description: The parameter position to measure the curve length to. This value must be within the range of the parameter extents as provided by getParameterExtents.

parameters.name: length
parameters.description: The output curve length between the from and to parameter positions on the curve.

getParameterAtLength.title: CurveEvaluator2D.getParameterAtLength Method
getParameterAtLength.description: Get the parameter position on the curve that is the specified curve length from the specified starting parameter position.
getParameterAtLength.return_description: Returns true if the parameter was successfully returned.

parameters.name: fromParameter
parameters.description: The parameter position to start measuring the curve length from. This value must be within the range of the parameter extents as provided by getParameterExtents.

parameters.name: length
parameters.description: The curve length to offset the from parameter by. A negative length value will offset in the negative parameter direction.

parameters.name: parameter
parameters.description: The output parameter value that is the specified curve length from the starting parameter position.

getParameterAtPoint.title: CurveEvaluator2D.getParameterAtPoint Method
getParameterAtPoint.description: Get the parameter position that correspond to a point on the curve. For reliable results, the point should lie on the curve within model tolerance. If the point does not lie on the curve, the parameter of the nearest point on the curve will generally be returned.
getParameterAtPoint.return_description: Returns true of the parameter was successfully returned.

parameters.name: point
parameters.description: The point to get the curve parameter value at.

parameters.name: parameter
parameters.description: The output parameter position corresponding to the point.

getParameterExtents.title: CurveEvaluator2D.getParameterExtents Method
getParameterExtents.description: Get the parametric range of the curve.
getParameterExtents.return_description: Returns true if the curve is bounded and the parameter extents were successfully returned.

parameters.name: startParameter
parameters.description: The output lower bound of the parameter range.

parameters.name: endParameter
parameters.description: The output upper bound of the parameter range.

getParametersAtPoints.title: CurveEvaluator2D.getParametersAtPoints Method
getParametersAtPoints.description: Get the parameter positions that correspond to a set of points on the curve. For reliable results, the points should lie on the curve within model tolerance. If the points do not lie on the curve, the parameter of the nearest point on the curve will generally be returned.
getParametersAtPoints.return_description: Returns true if the parameters were successfully returned.

parameters.name: points
parameters.description: An array of points to get the curve parameter values at.

parameters.name: parameters
parameters.description: The output array of parameter positions corresponding to the set of points. The length of this array will be equal to the length of the points array specified.

getPointAtParameter.title: CurveEvaluator2D.getPointAtParameter Method
getPointAtParameter.description: Get the point on the curve that corresponds to evaluating a parameter position on the curve.
getPointAtParameter.return_description: Returns true if the point was successfully returned.

parameters.name: parameter
parameters.description: The parameter position to evaluate the curve position at. The parameter value must be within the range of the parameter extents as provided by getParameterExtents.

parameters.name: point
parameters.description: The output curve position corresponding to evaluating the curve at that parameter position.

getPointsAtParameters.title: CurveEvaluator2D.getPointsAtParameters Method
getPointsAtParameters.description: Get the points on the curve that correspond to evaluating a set of parameter positions on the curve.
getPointsAtParameters.return_description: Returns true if the points were successfully returned.

parameters.name: parameters
parameters.description: The array of parameter positions to evaluate the curve position at. Each parameter value must be within the range of the parameter extents as provided by getParameterExtents.

parameters.name: points
parameters.description: The output array of curve positions corresponding to evaluating the curve at that parameter position. The length of this array will be equal to the length of the parameters array specified.

getSecondDerivative.title: CurveEvaluator2D.getSecondDerivative Method
getSecondDerivative.description: Get the second derivative of the curve at the specified parameter position.
getSecondDerivative.return_description: Returns true if the second derivative was successfully returned.

parameters.name: parameter
parameters.description: The parameter position to get the curve second derivative at. The parameter value must be within the range of the parameter extents as provided by getParameterExtents.

parameters.name: secondDerivative
parameters.description: The output second derivative vector at the parameter position specified.

getSecondDerivatives.title: CurveEvaluator2D.getSecondDerivatives Method
getSecondDerivatives.description: Get the second derivatives of the curve at the specified parameter positions.
getSecondDerivatives.return_description: Returns true if the second derivatives were successfully returned.

parameters.name: parameters
parameters.description: The array of parameter positions to get the curve second derivative at. Each parameter value must be within the range of the parameter extents as provided by getParameterExtents.

parameters.name: secondDerivatives
parameters.description: The output array of second derivative vectors at each parameter position specified. The length of this array is equal to the length of the parameters array specified.

getStrokes.title: CurveEvaluator2D.getStrokes Method
getStrokes.description: Get a sequence of points between two curve parameter positions. The points will be a linear interpolation along the curve between these two parameter positions where the maximum deviation between the curve and each line segment will not exceed the specified tolerance value.
getStrokes.return_description: Returns true if the interpolation points were successfully returned.

parameters.name: fromParameter
parameters.description: The starting parameter position to interpolate points from. The parameter value must be within the range of the parameter extents as provided by getParameterExtents.

parameters.name: toParameter
parameters.description: The ending parameter position to interpolate points to. The parameter value must be within the range of the parameter extents as provided by getParameterExtents.

parameters.name: tolerance
parameters.description: The maximum distance tolerance between the curve and the linear interpolation.

parameters.name: vertexCoordinates
parameters.description: The output array of linear interpolation points.

getTangent.title: CurveEvaluator2D.getTangent Method
getTangent.description: Get the tangent to the curve at a parameter position on the curve.
getTangent.return_description: Returns true if the tangent was successfully returned.

parameters.name: parameter
parameters.description: The parameter position to return the tangent at. This value must be within the range of the parameter extents as provided by getParameterExtents.

parameters.name: tangent
parameters.description: The output tangent vector at the curve position.

getTangents.title: CurveEvaluator2D.getTangents Method
getTangents.description: Get the tangent to the curve at a number of parameter positions on the curve.
getTangents.return_description: Returns true if the tangents were successfully returned.

parameters.name: parameters
parameters.description: The array of parameter positions to return the tangent at. Each parameter value must be within the range of the parameter extents as provided by getParameterExtents.

parameters.name: tangents
parameters.description: The output array of tangent vectors for each position on the curve. The length of this array will be the same as the length of the parameters array provided.

getThirdDerivative.title: CurveEvaluator2D.getThirdDerivative Method
getThirdDerivative.description: Get the third derivative of the curve at the specified parameter position.
getThirdDerivative.return_description: Returns true if the third derivative was successfully returned.

parameters.name: parameter
parameters.description: The parameter position to get the curve third derivative at. The parameter value must be within the range of the parameter extents as provided by getParameterExtents.

parameters.name: thirdDerivative
parameters.description: The output third derivative vector at the parameter position specified.

getThirdDerivatives.title: CurveEvaluator2D.getThirdDerivatives Method
getThirdDerivatives.description: Get the third derivatives of the curve at the specified parameter positions.
getThirdDerivatives.return_description: Returns true if the third derivatives were successfully returned.

parameters.name: parameters
parameters.description: The array of parameter positions to get the curve third derivative at. Each parameter value must be within the range of the parameter extents as provided by getParameterExtents.

parameters.name: thirdDerivatives
parameters.description: The output array of third derivative vectors at each parameter position specified. The length of this array is equal to the length of the parameters array specified.

isValid.title: CurveEvaluator2D.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = curveEvaluator2D_var.isValid

objectType.title: CurveEvaluator2D.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = curveEvaluator2D_var.objectType
